learning_objective: Students explain how trade winds, ocean temperatures, and atmospheric circulation interact during El Niño and La Niña phases.
specification:
Type: microsim **sim-id:** enso-cycle
**Library:** p5.js
**Status:** Specified **Bloom Level:** Understand **Bloom Verb:** Explain **Learning Objective:** Students explain how trade winds, ocean temperatures, and atmospheric circulation interact during El Niño and La Niña phases. **Instructional Rationale:** Interactive visualization of a dynamic cycle helps students see the system as a whole rather than memorizing isolated facts. **Visual Elements:** - Cross-section view of the Pacific Ocean from South America (right) to Australia/Indonesia (left) - Animated trade wind arrows showing direction and strength - Color-coded ocean temperature gradient (blue = cool, red = warm) - Thermocline line showing depth of warm/cool boundary - Cloud and rain icons showing precipitation patterns - Upwelling arrows along South American coast **Interactions:** - Toggle button: Normal / El Niño / La Niña - Animated transitions between states showing wind reversal, warm water migration, and thermocline changes - Hover over regions to see local effects (drought, flooding, temperature changes) - Speed slider for animation rate **Colors:** Ocean blue (#1a73e8) to warm red (#e63946) gradient, atmosphere light blue (#87CEEB), land green/brown
